name: Strait of Hormuz
detail: Fully closed. Commercial traffic near zero. Heavily mined with active drone/missile threats. Latest AIS data from vessel tracking shows no transits in past 72 hours. -15.0 mbpd disruption.

name: Qatar LNG (Ras Laffan)
detail: Extensive damage from mid-March strikes unrepaired. Force majeure remains in effect with no restart announced. Disruption estimated at 2.0 mbpd equivalent.

name: Abqaiq (Saudi Arabia)
detail: Operating normally with East-West bypass active at +4.5 mbpd offset. No new incidents reported in latest market updates.

name: Kharg Island (Iran)
detail: Exporting estimated 1.2-1.4 mbpd via shadow fleet to China. Military targets hit but core oil loading infrastructure remains functional.
update: Slight upward revision in export volume estimate from tanker tracking data.

name: Fujairah (UAE)
detail: Operations partially restored but still impacted by March drone strikes. Limited rerouting via Oman continues.

name: Ras Tanura (Saudi Arabia)
detail: Refinery running but sea exports constrained by Hormuz closure. Increased volumes confirmed rerouted to Yanbu terminal.
update: Rerouting to Yanbu now handling majority of exports per energy market reports.

name: Assaluyeh / South Pars (Iran)
detail: Treatment plants struck mid-March remain offline with no visible repair activity in recent imagery.

name: Bandar Abbas naval base
detail: Struck multiple times late March. IRGC Navy assets destroyed with pier facilities non-functional.

name: Kirkuk-Ceyhan Pipeline
detail: Flows currently estimated at 300 kbpd under Iraq-KRG agreement. On track to reach 450 kbpd in coming weeks as bypass route.
update: Flow rates increased from initial restart levels.

name: Bab el-Mandeb / Red Sea
detail: Houthi attacks ongoing in support of Iran. Shipping volumes remain sharply reduced with majority of vessels using Cape of Good Hope route.

name: Iranian Power Grid
detail: Multiple power plants including Isfahan, Bandar Abbas and Ahvaz struck. Estimated 45% of grid offline with widespread blackouts persisting.
update: Minor restoration in non-critical areas but overall status unchanged.

name: Fordow Enrichment Facility
detail: Facility approximately 55% offline following US strikes. Centrifuge halls severely damaged though some underground sections intact.

name: Bandar Abbas Naval Base
detail: Cratered by strikes with piers destroyed and multiple vessels sunk. No naval operations observed.

name: Natanz Enrichment Facility
detail: Impacted by reported strikes in late March. Centrifuge operations estimated at 40% capacity with significant infrastructure damage.
update: New facility added based on OSINT and news reporting of additional strikes.

name: Yanbu Terminal (Saudi Arabia)
detail: Handling increased crude and product exports as Hormuz bypass. No direct attacks reported; operating at elevated throughput.
update: New facility added due to confirmed increased utilization as alternative export route.
